question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: hapiVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile with hapiVersionMajor=null, hapiVersionMinor=null, hapiVersionPatch=null so that hapiVersion() returns HAPI_VERSION_NOT_SET (0.0.0), causing downstream version-gated authorization checks to fall into the oldest/most-permissive code path and accept transactions that lack required signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: hapiVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user supply hapiVersionMajor=0, hapiVersionMinor=22, hapiVersionPatch=0 to force the lazy-computed hapiVersion below HAPI_VERSION_0_23_0, bypassing the post-0.23 requirement that child contract creations carry their own signer authorization, thereby allowing unsigned child contract transactions to be ingested as authorized?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: hapiVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user set hapiVersionMajor=0, hapiVersionMinor=26, hapiVersionPatch=0 to stay below HAPI_VERSION_0_27_0, causing staking-related authorization checks (e.g., CryptoUpdate staked account changes) to be skipped, so that account ownership changes proceed without the required key signature?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: hapiVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user set hapiVersionMajor=0, hapiVersionMinor=46, hapiVersionPatch=0 to stay below HAPI_VERSION_0_47_0, causing token metadata key authorization checks to be skipped, so that token metadata is updated without the metadata key owner's signature?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: hapiVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user set hapiVersionMajor=0, hapiVersionMinor=48, hapiVersionPatch=0 to stay below HAPI_VERSION_0_49_0, bypassing the metadata key authorization gate in TokenCreateTransactionHandler and TokenUpdateTransactionHandler, allowing token metadata changes without the token owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: hapiVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user set hapiVersionMajor=0, hapiVersionMinor=52, hapiVersionPatch=0 to stay below HAPI_VERSION_0_53_0, causing the mirror node to apply a legacy authorization code path that does not enforce the newer signer requirements introduced in 0.53.0?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: isEmpty()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the isEmpty() check to return true by injecting a reference-equal EMPTY singleton into the processing pipeline, causing the parser to skip all signature and authorization validation for the entire RecordFile batch, effectively authorizing all contained transactions without signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: clear()] Can an unprivileged external user cause clear() to be called on a RecordFile that still has items pending authorization checks, since clear() only nulls logsBloom and empties sidecars but does NOT clear items, resulting in partially-cleared state where sidecar-based authorization evidence is lost but transactions proceed as authorized?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: copy()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shallow copy produced by copy() (via toBuilder().build()) so that the shared items List<RecordItem> reference is mutated after the copy is made, injecting unsigned transactions into the copy's item list that bypass the original authorization checks performed on the source RecordFile?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: copy()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that copy() produces a shallow copy where the items list is shared between original and copy, so that modifying the items in the copy (e.g., replacing a RecordItem's signatureMap) retroactively affects the original, causing the authorization state of already-processed transactions to be corrupted?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: previousHash] Can an unprivileged external user supply an empty string ('') as previousHash in a RecordFile, exploiting the verifyHashChain logic that passes when expectedPrevHash is empty, to inject a RecordFile with fabricated transactions at the start of the chain without any prior authorization chain validation?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: previousHash] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile whose previousHash matches the all-zeros genesis hash (000...000), causing the hash chain verifier to accept it as the first file in the chain, thereby injecting unauthorized transactions that appear to have full network authorization?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: hash] Can an unprivileged external user set the hash field to a value that matches the fileHash field (as done in pre-V5 readers), exploiting the dual-use of hash/fileHash in older format versions to bypass the separate metadata hash verification that guards transaction authorization integrity?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: fileHash] Can an unprivileged external user supply a RecordFile where fileHash is set to a known-valid hash from a legitimate file but the items list contains different (unauthorized) transactions, exploiting any gap between when fileHash is verified against the signature file and when items are parsed, to authorize transactions that were never signed by network nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: metadataHash] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Transient annotation on metadataHash (meaning it is never persisted to the database) to supply a null or forged metadataHash, causing the metadata hash verification step in NodeSignatureVerifier to be skipped (since metadataHashSignature check is conditional on non-null), thereby accepting a RecordFile whose metadata integrity—and thus transaction authorization chain—has not been verified?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: metadataHash] Can an unprivileged external user cause metadataHash to be null (since it is @Transient and @JsonIgnore) when a RecordFile is deserialized from JSON or reconstructed from the database, so that the conditional metadata signature check in NodeSignatureVerifier.verifySignature() is bypassed, allowing a tampered file with unauthorized transactions to pass verification?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: version] Can an unprivileged external user set version to a value below ProtoRecordFileReader.VERSION (6) to force the importer to use a legacy RecordFileReader that does not perform metadata hash verification, thereby bypassing the authorization chain check that protects against tampered transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: version] Can an unprivileged external user set version=2 (the oldest supported format) to trigger AbstractPreV5RecordFileReader which sets hash=fileHash (no separate metadata hash), eliminating the metadata hash authorization check and allowing a RecordFile with forged transaction signatures to be accepted as fully authorized?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: items] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @EqualsAndHashCode.Exclude annotation on items to inject additional unauthorized RecordItem objects into the items list after equality/hash checks have been performed, so that the extra transactions are processed and persisted as authorized without ever being part of the verified hash chain?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: items] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @JsonIgnore annotation on items to supply a RecordFile via a JSON API endpoint where items is not deserialized from the request, then separately inject items through another code path, causing transactions to be processed without the JSON-layer authorization checks that would normally apply?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: items] Can an unprivileged external user supply a RecordFile with count=0 but a non-empty items list (since count and items are independent fields), causing the parser to record zero transactions in metrics/DB while still processing all items in the forEach loop, effectively hiding unauthorized transactions from audit trails?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: count] Can an unprivileged external user set count to a value larger than the actual items list size, causing the RecordFileParser's count tracking to be inflated, potentially masking the fact that some items were injected without proper signer authorization by making the file appear to contain more verified transactions than it does?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: consensusEnd] Can an unprivileged external user set consensusEnd to a timestamp that falls outside the date range filter window, causing the RecordFileParser's dateRangeFilter to skip all items in the file, but still have the RecordFile itself persisted to the database as a processed block, creating a gap in the authorization audit trail?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: consensusStart] Can an unprivileged external user set consensusStart to a value greater than consensusEnd, creating an invalid time window that confuses the date range filter into either processing all items or none, potentially allowing unauthorized transactions to slip through or legitimate ones to be skipped?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: digestAlgorithm] Can an unprivileged external user set digestAlgorithm to a weak or null value, causing the hash computation to use a weaker algorithm that is easier to forge, allowing the attacker to produce a valid-looking fileHash for a RecordFile containing transactions that were never approved by the required signers?
